summary: Large gm_16F1 decompilation pass focused on match-result/decision and bonus/award logic. The PR replaced several placeholders with C, tightened function prototypes, expanded game/player result structs, and added static data needed for matching. decomp.dev reported +2080 matched bytes overall, with 5 new full matches and several high-percentage partial improvements in gm_16F1.
what_changed: Implemented multiple gm_16F1 functions that were previously placeholders, including fn_8016F39C, fn_8016F548, fn_8016F740, fn_8016F9A8, fn_8016FAD4, fn_8016FFD4, fn_801701C0, fn_80171BA4, and fn_80171DC4.

Updated gm_16F1.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or incorrect types to concrete signatures, including s32 fn_8016F1F0(int), int fn_8016F39C(HSD_Text**, void*, u8, u16, u8, u8), int fn_8016F548(void*, u16, u8, u8), int fn_8016F740(void*, u16, u8, u8), int fn_8016F9A8(void*, u16, u8, u8), int fn_8016FAD4(struct lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t*, u32, int, u8), int fn_8016FFD4(struct lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t*, int, u8), int fn_80171BA4(void*), and void fn_80171DC4(void).
Changed gm_16AE.c call site fn_8016C7D0 so fn_80171DC4 is called without passing the HSD_GObj argument, matching the updated void prototype.
Added includes in gm_16F1.c for gm_16AE.h, plbonuslib.h, pl/types.h, and player.h to support newly decompiled logic.

Added static data declarations in gm_16F1.static.h, including lbl_804DA2E8/lbl_804DA2EC/lbl_804DA2EE, lbl_804DA2F0/lbl_804DA2F4/lbl_804DA2F6, lbl_804DA2F8, lbl_804DA2FC, and struct lbl_803B7A60_t initialized with zero/default arrays.
Expanded struct lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t in gm/types.h with x7 and fields inside x58 entries including xA, x40, and adjusted padding around x8, x30, and x44.
Expanded pl_804D6470_t in pl/types.h by splitting padding at +0x138 and naming unsigned int fields x13C and x140.
Adjusted gmregclear fn_8017F1B8 mask comparison by storing fn_8016F180(i) in an output variable and checking output & mask, contributing to a full match.
smart_moves: Centralized repeated table-flag lookup through fn_8016F180 in paths such as fn_8016F870 and fn_80170110 instead of open-coding a scan of lbl_803D5A4C each time.
Separated regular player-stat checks from extended result checks: kinds below 0xD7 use pl_80039418, while kinds at or above 0xD7 use fn_801701C0.
Preserved matching-sensitive stack behavior with PAD_STACK calls after returns in several functions, including fn_8016F39C, fn_8016F548, fn_8016F740, fn_8016F9A8, and fn_8016FFD4.
Introduced a static zero/default table struct lbl_803B7A60_t to model repeated default arrays used by fn_801701C0 rather than scattering magic initializer values.
Updated call sites and headers together when fn_80171DC4 was discovered to take no HSD_GObj argument, preventing signature drift.
Used explicit u8/u16/s32 casts and temporary locals for masks, rankings, and score calculations, which is often necessary for Melee MWCC matching.
decomp_lessons: When a result/bonus routine has many similar cases, first identify shared ranking and score setup; fn_801701C0 computes rankings once and then switches over special result kinds.
For decomping gm result logic, distinguish normal bonus IDs from special decision/result IDs by threshold checks such as kind < 0xD7.
Struct layout progress can unlock function progress: adding xA and x40 inside lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t::x58 made death/suicide/score formulas expressible in C.
Prototype corrections matter for matching beyond the local file; changing fn_80171DC4 to void required fixing gm_16AE.c.
Small expression-order changes can fully match a nearly matched function: gmregclear fn_8017F1B8 improved by computing output = (u8) fn_8016F180(i) and then checking output & mask.
Locale-sensitive UI/data handling appeared in fn_8016F39C: item id 0xDE maps to 0x102 when lbLang_IsSettingUS() is true.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions retained address-based names such as fn_8016F39C, fn_801701C0, fn_80171BA4, and fn_80171DC4.
Unknown static data retained lbl_ address names such as lbl_803D5A4C, lbl_803B7A60, lbl_804D65A0, lbl_804D65A8, lbl_804D65B0, and lbl_804DA2F0.
Struct fields remained offset-style names such as x0, x5, xA, x20, x24, x40, pad3F0, and x58 when semantic names were not yet proven.
Function parameters used mixed generic and tentative semantic names; examples include arg0/arg1 for unclear values and mask/player_id/player/rules/kind/flags where usage was clearer.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK with explicit byte counts to reproduce stack frame shape in otherwise valid C functions.
Used do/while loops for fixed player loops in fn_80171BA4, matching patterns over 6 player slots.
Used pointer-walking locals over arrays, including score_ptr, x58_p, sc_p, rk_p, and struct lbl_8046B6A0_24C_58_t* p/q, to guide codegen.
Used typed bulk copies through casts for compact local initialization, such as assigning rankings from lbl_804DA2E8/lbl_804DA2EC/lbl_804DA2EE and a local typedef copy_t in fn_801701C0.
Used explicit unsigned casts in conditions such as (unsigned) fn_801701C0(...) != 0 and (u32) index bounds checks.
Used branch factoring around mask checks and helper calls to match compiler output, notably output & mask in gmregclear fn_8017F1B8.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
decomp.dev reported matched code improved to 63.80%, +0.05%, +2080 bytes.
decomp.dev reported 5 new full matches: gm_16F1 fn_80171DC4 (+752), fn_8016F548 (+504), fn_8016F740 (+304), fn_8016F9A8 (+300), and gmregclear fn_8017F1B8 (+6).
decomp.dev reported 6 improved unmatched items: gm_16F1 fn_801701C0 to 91.77%, fn_8016FAD4 to 95.15%, fn_80171BA4 to 94.43%, fn_8016F39C to 92.99%, fn_8016FFD4 to 99.30%, and .sdata2 to 50.00%.
decomp.dev reported one unrelated-looking regression in gmresult fn_80174468, from 89.84% to 89.49%, -4 bytes.
follow_up_queries: Investigate remaining non-matching portions of gm_16F1 fn_801701C0, especially switch cases and local initialization patterns.
Investigate remaining non-matching portions of fn_8016FAD4, fn_80171BA4, fn_8016F39C, and fn_8016FFD4.
Check whether lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t::x58 fields xA, x20, x24, and x40 can receive semantic names from result-screen or player-stat usage.
Audit the reported gmresult fn_80174468 -4 byte regression to see whether it was caused by header/prototype or struct-layout changes.
Look for opportunities to replace remaining arg0/arg1 parameters with semantic names once gm_16F1 result rules are better understood.
